next_steps: Examine tracheal aspirate for rhabditiform larvae of Strongyloides stercoralis
Perform punch biopsy of cutaneous purpuric lesions for histopathology and tissue culture
Order stool studies for ova and parasites (specifically concentrated for Strongyloides)
Send serum for Histoplasma antigen and galactomannan testing
Obtain Bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) for broad-spectrum microbiological, fungal, and PCR testing
Start empiric treatment for hyperinfection syndrome (e.g., ivermectin) until ruled out
Perform serial abdominal exams and imaging to monitor for bowel perforation or progression of ileus
